qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ASGI handler reads unlimited request body without enforcing DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns unbounded ASGI request-body buffering in ASGIHandler.read_body(), while the advisory concerns SQL injection in FilteredRelation column aliases containing control characters when passed through QuerySet method kwargs.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or equivalent root cause. The advisory is Django CVE-2026-1287/GHSA-gvg8-93h5-g6qq, an SQL injection issue in FilteredRelation column aliases via control characters in expanded kwargs to QuerySet methods such as annotate(), aggregate(), extra(), values(), values_list(), and alias(). The only final qualifying finding describes a separate ASGI request-body resource exhaustion issue in django/core/handlers/asgi.py.
evidence: Advisory details name FilteredRelation SQL injection in column aliases via control characters and crafted dictionary expansion into QuerySet methods.
Session manifest lists asgi_unlimited_body_read as the only final qualifying finding.

asgi_unlimited_body_read/vulnerability_report.json identifies ASGIHandler.read_body() unbounded buffering to SpooledTemporaryFile as the root cause, with no FilteredRelation, ORM alias, control-character, or SQL injection source-to-sink path.
